title: GitLab (NASDAQ: GTLB) launches workforce cuts and AI push while reaffirming FY27 guidance
text: GitLab announced a significant restructuring initiative while maintaining its fiscal 2027 outlook. The company plans to reduce the number of countries by up to 30% where it maintains small teams, and will reorganize research and development into approximately 60 smaller, more autonomous units. The restructuring includes flattening organizational layers and implementing AI agents to automate internal processes. Management indicated that the majority of the resulting savings from the plan will be reinvested into strategic growth and AI infrastructure rather than serving as pure cost reduction. Despite these substantial operational changes, GitLab reaffirmed both first-quarter and full-year fiscal 2027 guidance issued on March 3, 2026. The company positioned the restructuring as preparation for the agentic era of software development, where artificial intelligence plays a central role in code creation and deployment. Financial details regarding the restructuring's scope and impact will be disclosed during the company's June 2, 2026 earnings call. CEO William Staples emphasized that the transformation represents strategic repositioning rather than a conventional cost-cutting exercise.
